evals.prompt: /brainstorm ideas for a farewell gift for a coworker who's leaving after 5 years
evals.expected_output: 3-cluster ideation (Wild/Core/Seed), punchy headlines + one-liners, whiteboard energy, closing invitation line
expectations: Output has three clearly labeled clusters: Wild Ideas, Core Ideas, Seed Ideas
Each idea has a short headline (roughly 3-7 words) followed by one sentence, not a paragraph
Tone is energetic and casual, not a formal numbered report
Response ends with an invitation to go deeper (e.g. asking which idea to dive into)
No idea is hedged with disclaimers like 'this might not work but'

evals.prompt: let's brainstorm ways to cut our CI pipeline build time — right now a full run takes 22 minutes and it's killing our iteration speed
evals.expected_output: Technical brainstorm reflecting engineering context — Wild Ideas as architectural bets, Core Ideas as concrete engineering approaches, Seed Ideas as experiments worth prototyping
expectations: Ideas are grounded in the stated context (CI pipeline, 22-minute build time) rather than generic advice
Core Ideas are concrete and technically actionable, not vague platitudes
Total idea count is roughly 7-9, not an exhaustive list of 15+
Response ends with an invitation to go deeper or shift direction
Does not turn into a research report or ask clarifying questions when the topic is already clear enough to run with

evals.prompt: give me ideas for a birthday party theme, and go wilder with it
evals.expected_output: Adapted mode: Wild Ideas cluster dominates, Core Ideas cluster is dropped per the skill's mode-handling instructions
expectations: Core Ideas cluster is absent or minimal, since 'go wilder' should drop it per the skill's mode rules
Wild Ideas cluster is expanded relative to a normal run (more than the default 2-3 items, or clearly the dominant section)
Ideas are unconventional/surprising rather than safe or practical
Response still ends with an invitation to continue (dive in, riff further, or shift direction)
The mode switch happens without the skill announcing or explaining that it's switching modes
